title: Jeffreya palustris

uses: Jeffreya palustris, also known as marsh pennyroyal, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments. side_effects: Jeffreya palustris, also known as marsh pennyroyal, contains essential oils that can cause various side effects when ingested or applied topically. intro: Jeffreya palustris, also known as sawyer or wild marjoram, is a perennial herb belonging to the Lamiaceae family. The plant is valued for its various health benefits, including its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues, relieve pain, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of J. palustris include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Common herbal preparations made from J. palustris include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
